tools.name: analyze_payment_3ds
tools.description: Pure local analyzer for a Payment's 3DS (Strong Customer Authentication) state. Pass a payment_id (string) and the tool fetches the Payment then derives { status: 'not_required'|'frictionless'|'challe

tools.name: calculate_installments
tools.description: Calculate cuotas (installments) options for a given amount. THE killer Argentine feature — returns options like '12 cuotas sin interés de $X' (recommended_message field) which you should surface VERBA

input.bin: string? 6-8 chars (first digits of card for issuer-specific promos)

output.offers: array of {payment_method_id, payment_type_id, issuer_name, options: [{installments, installment_amount, total_amount, installment_rate, recommended_message}]}

tools.name: cancel_order
tools.description: Cancel an Order. Releases any auth-holds and marks the Order as canceled. For orders that have already been CAPTURED, use refund_payment instead — cancel only works pre-capture. **IRREVERSIBLE — confi

tools.name: cancel_payment
tools.description: Cancel a pending or in_process payment (only works before approval). Once approved, use refund_payment instead. Common use: cancel an unpaid ticket payment that's still pending. **IRREVERSIBLE — confi

tools.name: cancel_point_payment_intent
tools.description: Cancel an OPEN point payment intent before the buyer interacts with the device. ONLY WORKS while state='OPEN' — once the buyer taps, you can't cancel; refund_payment after the fact instead. **IRREVERS

tools.name: cancel_qr_payment
tools.description: Cancel a pending QR order on a POS. Necessary if the buyer never scans — otherwise the next create_qr_payment on the same POS returns 409. **IRREVERSIBLE — but low-stakes since the QR has not been pai

tools.name: cancel_subscription
tools.description: Cancel an active Mercado Pago subscription. After cancellation, MP will not charge the customer again. This action is irreversible — confirm with the user before calling.

tools.name: capture_order
tools.description: Capture a previously-authorized Order (only for orders created with capture_mode='manual'). Captures up to the originally-authorized amount; pass amount for partial capture. Common use: ride-share mar

tools.name: capture_payment
tools.description: Capture an authorized credit-card payment that was created with capture=false. Use for hold-then-capture flows (e.g., authorize on order, capture on shipment). Optional partial amount. **MOVES MONEY —

tools.name: charge_saved_card
tools.description: Charge a previously-saved card for a returning customer. Requires customer_id + card_id (from list_customer_cards) AND a fresh CVV the user provides this session. AR Mercado Pago does NOT support CVV-

tools.name: compute_marketplace_fee
tools.description: PURE HELPER (no network) — given a transaction amount + fee rule (% or flat ARS, with optional min/max floors), returns the exact `marketplace_fee` value in ARS to pass to create_order or create_payme

tools.name: confirm_3ds_challenge
tools.description: After the buyer completes a 3DS challenge (redirected back from challengeUrl), call this to poll MP and confirm whether the payment is now resolved. Polls get_payment up to N times with exponential ba

tools.name: create_customer
tools.description: Create a Mercado Pago customer record so the buyer can save cards for future charges. Idempotent on email — if a customer with that email exists, MP returns it instead of creating a duplicate. Use fin

tools.name: create_customer_card
tools.description: Add a saved card to an existing customer using a card_token (one-time token from MP frontend Cardform — agents should NEVER take raw card data, that's a PCI violation). Returns the saved CustomerCard

tools.name: create_order
tools.description: Create a new Order via MP's modern Order Management API. DIFFERENT from create_payment_preference: Order is a transactional entity with explicit lifecycle (created → processed → captured/canceled), su

tools.name: create_payment
tools.description: Create a one-time payment. Two flows: (a) with a card token from MP frontend Cardform — for transparent checkout; (b) without token, for non-card methods like 'account_money', 'rapipago', 'pagofacil'.

output.status_detail: string (see AGENTS.md for recovery actions per value)

tools.name: create_payment_preference
tools.description: Create a Mercado Pago Checkout Pro preference and get back a payment URL (init_point) to send to the customer. THIS is the recommended way for an agent to take a payment when you only have a payer ema

input.items: array of {title, quantity, unit_price, description?, picture_url?}

output.sandbox_init_point_url: string (sandbox — use this if your token is TEST-)

tools.name: create_point_payment_intent
tools.description: Create a payment intent on a physical Point device — the device prompts the buyer to tap/insert/swipe their card. Returns immediately with intent_id; query state via get_point_payment_intent or wait f

tools.name: create_pos
tools.description: Create a POS (Point of Sale) under a store. The POS's external_id is what create_qr_payment uses. Each physical checkout / counter / agent typically has its own POS. Categories are MP-defined (default

tools.name: create_qr_payment
tools.description: Generate a dynamic in-store QR for a buyer to scan with any AR wallet (Modo, BNA+, Cuenta DNI, Naranja X, Mercado Pago, etc. — interop is mandated by Transferencias 3.0). Requires a pre-configured POS

tools.name: create_store
tools.description: Create a store under the seller's MP account. Stores are the parent entity for POSes (which generate QR payments). Required ONE-TIME setup before create_pos. Pass a unique external_id and a display na

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.description: Create a Mercado Pago recurring subscription. Returns an init_point URL where the customer must complete the FIRST payment with their card and CVV (this is a hard MP requirement; agents cannot bypass

output.init_point_url: string (URL the buyer must visit to complete first payment)

tools.name: create_subscription_plan
tools.description: Create a REUSABLE subscription plan (preapproval_plan). Different from create_subscription: a plan defines price + frequency once, then customers subscribe to it via subscribe_to_plan. Use plans for S

tools.name: create_webhook
tools.description: Subscribe a webhook URL to a MP topic (payment, subscription_authorized_payment, subscription_preapproval, merchant_order, point_integration_wh). MP will POST to this URL when events of that topic fir

tools.name: delete_customer_card
tools.description: Delete a saved card from a customer. Common use: customer requests removal, or expired card cleanup. **IRREVERSIBLE — confirm with the user before calling. The customer must re-enter card data (PAN +

tools.name: delete_pos
tools.description: Delete a POS. IRREVERSIBLE. Cancels any pending QR orders attached to it. Confirm with user before calling.

tools.name: delete_store
tools.description: Delete a store. IRREVERSIBLE. Confirm with user before calling. Will fail if the store has associated POSes — delete those first.

tools.name: delete_webhook
tools.description: Delete a webhook subscription. MP stops POSTing to it immediately. **IRREVERSIBLE — confirm before calling. State the webhook URL + topic so the user knows which subscription is being removed. Re-subs

tools.name: explain_payment_status
tools.description: PURE HELPER (no network) — given a Payment object (from get_payment / create_payment / handle_webhook), returns { summary, recommendedAction, final, paid, retryable } in Spanish. Translates MP's crypt

tools.name: find_applicable_promos
tools.description: PURE HELPER (no network, sub-ms) — returns the 'cuotas sin interés' promotions applicable to a given (issuer, paymentMethodId, amount, category, date) tuple. Includes the federal Ahora 3/6/12/18/24/30

tools.name: find_customer_by_email
tools.description: Find an existing customer by email address. Returns the customer object if found, or null. Use before create_customer to avoid duplicate records.

tools.name: get_account_balance
tools.description: Get the seller's current MP wallet balance. Returns { available_balance, unavailable_balance, total_amount, currency_id }. The available balance is what the seller can withdraw or pay with right now;

tools.name: get_account_info
tools.description: Get info about the Mercado Pago account that owns the access token: site_id (MLA=Argentina), country_id, user_type (registered, partial, etc.). Useful to verify the agent is connected to the right acc

tools.name: get_customer
tools.description: Get a customer by id. Returns full Customer object: email, first_name, last_name, identification, address, default_card, registered cards. PURE READ. USE WHEN you have the customer_id from a previous

tools.name: get_customer_card
tools.description: Get details of a single saved card by (customer_id, card_id). Returns last 4 digits, expiration, brand, issuer. PURE READ — useful before charge_saved_card to confirm the card is still valid.

tools.name: get_dispute
tools.description: Get details of a specific dispute including reason, amount, resolution status. Read-only.

tools.name: get_merchant_order
tools.description: Get a merchant_order with all its associated payments + shipments. MerchantOrder is the parent entity for Payments associated with a single Preference — one Order can have multiple partial Payments (r

tools.name: get_order
tools.description: Fetch an Order by ID. Returns the Order with its lifecycle status and any attached payments/refunds.

tools.name: get_payment
tools.description: Fetch a single payment by ID. Use to confirm status after webhook arrives, or to inspect details (status_detail explains rejections).
tools.output: Same shape as create_payment output, plus net_received

tools.name: get_payment_preference
tools.description: Fetch a Checkout Pro preference by ID. Returns the preference config and current init_point URLs. Use to inspect a previously-created link.
tools.output: Same as create_payment_preference output, plus items array

tools.name: get_point_payment_intent
tools.description: Get the current state of a Point payment intent (OPEN, PROCESSING, FINISHED, CANCELED, ERROR). USE in polling loops if you can't wait for the webhook. When state=FINISHED, the intent.payment.id is the

tools.name: get_pos
tools.description: Fetch a POS by id. Returns: name, store_id, category, external_id, qr_template (if configured). PURE READ. Use when you need to find the external_id for create_qr_payment.

tools.name: get_refund
tools.description: Fetch a single refund by (payment_id, refund_id). Returns the Refund object with amount, status, date_created. PURE READ — useful to verify a refund processed or to reconcile partial-refund history.

tools.name: get_settlement
tools.description: Get details of a single settlement: amount, date_scheduled, date_processed, bank_account info (CBU + bank name).

tools.name: get_store
tools.description: Fetch a single store by (user_id, store_id). Returns store details: name, location, business_hours, external_id. PURE READ.

tools.name: get_subscription_plan
tools.description: Fetch a subscription plan by id. Returns plan config: amount, frequency, status, init_point. Use to inspect a plan before subscribing customers, or to display plan details to the user.

tools.name: get_subscription_status
tools.description: Check the current status of a Mercado Pago subscription. Use this to confirm the customer completed the first payment (status becomes 'authorized') or to inspect the next charge date.

tools.name: get_test_cards
tools.description: Pure helper that returns the official MP test cards for AR (MLA): VISA/Mastercard/Amex credit + debit, with the 'magic' holder names that route the payment to specific status_detail values (APRO=appro

tools.name: handle_webhook
tools.description: Process an incoming MP webhook in ONE call: verify the HMAC-SHA256 signature, parse the event, and (optionally) auto-fetch the underlying resource (Payment, Subscription, Order). Returns the structure

input.raw_body: string (raw JSON body — do NOT re-stringify)

tools.name: list_account_movements
tools.description: List wallet movements (incoming payments, transfers, refunds, holdings) for the active MP account. Filter by date range with `from`/`to` (ISO 8601). Useful for monthly conciliation or 'show me what ca

tools.name: list_bank_accounts
tools.description: List the bank accounts (CBUs) the seller has registered with MP for receiving payouts. Returns an array — the one with `is_default: true` is where settlements (release_money) go. USE BEFORE list_settl

tools.name: list_customer_cards
tools.description: List the saved cards for a customer. Returns array with last 4 digits, expiration, payment method (visa, master, naranja, etc.). The card_id can be used in subsequent create_payment calls to charge a

output.cards: array of {card_id, last_four_digits, expiration_month, expiration_year, payment_method, payment_method_name}

tools.name: list_identification_types
tools.description: List valid identification types for the seller's site. AR returns: DNI, CI, LE, LC, Otro, Pasaporte, CUIT, CUIL with their min/max length. Useful to validate an identification before passing to create

tools.name: list_issuers
tools.description: List card issuers (banks) that support a payment_method_id. Optionally filter by `bin` (first 6 digits of the card) for accurate issuer detection. Useful with calculate_installments — issuer-specific

tools.name: list_payment_disputes
tools.description: List all disputes / chargebacks raised against a payment. Read-only — resolution is dashboard-only. Surface the dashboard URL `https://www.mercadopago.com.ar/disputes/{dispute_id}` to the user when th

tools.name: list_payment_methods
tools.description: List all payment methods enabled for the seller's MP account (visa, master, naranja, naranja_x, cabal, account_money, rapipago, pagofacil, etc.). Use to validate which methods you can offer the custom

output.methods: array of {id, name, payment_type, status, min_amount, max_amount}

tools.name: list_point_devices
tools.description: List the physical Point devices (Smart, Tap to Pay, etc.) linked to the seller's MP account. Distinct from logical POS — these are actual terminals at brick-and-mortar shops. Returns each device's id

tools.name: list_pos
tools.description: List all POSes for the seller (or filtered by store_id). Use to find an existing POS before create_qr_payment, or to surface options.

tools.name: list_refunds
tools.description: List all refunds for a given payment. Returns array of Refund objects. Useful to confirm a refund was processed or to inspect partial-refund history.

tools.name: list_settlements
tools.description: List settlements (release_money) — i.e. transfers from the MP wallet to the seller's registered bank account (CBU). USE WHEN the user asks 'cuándo me deposita MP' or for monthly bank-conciliation repo

tools.name: list_settlements_all
tools.description: Collect ALL settlements matching a filter — auto-paginates. Pass `max_items` to cap. Use for monthly bank-conciliation reports.

tools.name: list_stores
tools.description: List all stores configured for this MP account. Use this to find an existing store_id before create_pos, or to surface store options to the agent.

tools.name: list_subscription_payments
tools.description: List the auto-charge attempts (authorized_payments) under a subscription. Useful for 'show me the cobros del último mes for this client' or to debug a failing recurring charge.

tools.name: list_subscription_plans
tools.description: List all subscription plans defined for this MP account. Useful before create_subscription_plan to check if one already exists, or for surfacing options to a customer.

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description: List all webhook subscriptions configured for this MP application. Use to see what topics + URLs are wired before adding new ones.

tools.name: mp_health_check
tools.description: Liveness probe against MP. Returns { ok, latencyMs, userId, circuit }. USE THIS as the first call in long-running agent workflows to verify (a) network path to MP is up, (b) accessToken is valid, (c)

tools.name: oauth_authorize_url
tools.description: Build the URL the SELLER (third-party MP account) visits to authorize your marketplace app. Pass the seller's redirect uri (must be whitelisted in MP dev panel) and an opaque state token (CSRF protect

input.redirect_uri: string (must be whitelisted in MP dev panel)
input.state: string (CSRF token, bind to user session)

tools.name: oauth_exchange_code
tools.description: Exchange the authorization code (from the OAuth redirect) for an `OAuthToken`. Returns access_token, refresh_token, user_id, and expires_in. **PERSIST the entire response** — refresh_token is long-liv

input.redirect_uri: string (must EXACTLY match the URL used in oauth_authorize_url)

tools.name: oauth_refresh_token
tools.description: Refresh a per-seller access_token using the saved refresh_token. Call PROACTIVELY before expires_in elapses, or REACTIVELY on a 401 from a per-seller MercadoPagoClient. Returns a fresh OAuthToken — pe

tools.name: pause_subscription
tools.description: Pause an authorized Mercado Pago subscription. Charges stop until resumed. Only works on subscriptions in 'authorized' status.

tools.name: refund_payment
tools.description: Refund an approved payment. Pass amount for partial refund; omit for full refund. Idempotency key is auto-generated based on paymentId+amount to prevent double-refunds on retries. **IRREVERSIBLE AND M

tools.name: register_bank_account
tools.description: Register a new bank account (CBU) for the seller. NOTE: MP usually requires this through the dashboard for compliance — this endpoint may not work for all accounts. If it fails with 403, redirect the

tools.name: resume_subscription
tools.description: Resume a paused Mercado Pago subscription. Charges resume on the next scheduled date. Only works on subscriptions in 'paused' status.

tools.name: search_merchant_orders
tools.description: Search merchant_orders by preference_id, external_reference, or status. Paginated. Returns up to 50 per page. USE WHEN you have a preference_id and want all its derived merchant_orders, or when reconc

tools.name: search_payments
tools.description: Search payments with filters. Most common: by external_reference (your-system identifier) to find all payments for an order, or by status='approved' to list successful charges in a date range. Returns

output.payments: array of {payment_id, status, amount, currency, payer_email, external_reference, date_created}

tools.name: search_payments_all
tools.description: Collect ALL payments matching a filter — auto-paginates under the hood. Returns an array (NOT paginated) so the agent doesn't have to manage offset/limit loops manually. SAFETY: pass `max_items` to ca

tools.name: search_subscriptions
tools.description: Search subscriptions across the seller's account. Filter by status (pending/authorized/paused/cancelled), payer_email, external_reference, or preapproval_plan_id (to find all subscribers of a plan). P

tools.name: subscribe_to_plan
tools.description: Subscribe a customer to an existing reusable plan. Returns a Preapproval with init_point URL where the customer completes first payment. Cleaner than create_subscription when you have fixed tiers.

tools.name: update_customer
tools.description: Update a customer's profile (first_name, last_name, phone, identification, address, default_card). MP merges the patch — fields you don't send remain unchanged. Use to keep customer records in sync (e

tools.name: update_merchant_order
tools.description: Update a merchant_order — typically to add items or shipping info. Most agent flows don't need this; use only when integrating with a custom shipping flow that requires updating the MO mid-lifecycle.

tools.name: update_order
tools.description: Patch an existing Order before it's captured/canceled. Common use: update items or external_reference.

tools.name: update_payment_preference
tools.description: Update a Checkout Pro preference (notification_url, back_urls, items, payer info, payment_methods exclusion list). Only works on preferences NOT yet paid. Common use: regenerate the link with a new no

tools.name: update_point_device_mode
tools.description: Switch a Point device's operating_mode between 'PDV' (bound to a logical POS, takes payments triggered through that POS) and 'STANDALONE' (works independently, accepts any payment). PDV is for cash-re

tools.name: update_pos
tools.description: Update a POS's properties (name, category, external_id). MP merges the patch.

tools.name: update_store
tools.description: Update a store's properties (name, location, business_hours, external_id). MP merges the patch.

tools.name: update_subscription
tools.description: Update a subscription's amount, status, reason, external_reference, OR card_token_id (to switch payment method when the buyer's card is expired/declined). For card swap: pass card_token_id from a fres

tools.name: update_subscription_plan
tools.description: Update a subscription plan's reason / amount / status / back_url. Existing customer subscriptions to the plan are NOT automatically updated — only NEW subscribers get the new pricing.

tools.name: update_webhook
tools.description: Update a webhook's URL or topic. Useful when you change deployment URLs without resubscribing from scratch.

tools.name: validate_tax_id
tools.description: PURE HELPER (no network, sub-ms) — validates a tax ID against the appropriate country algorithm. Supports AR (DNI/CUIT/CUIL with modulo-11), BR (CPF/CNPJ with two-step weighted modulo), MX (RFC struct
